mechanism: SL binds D14 alpha/beta hydrolase, induces conformational change and formation of D14-D3-D53 complex
evidence_sentence: D53 was shown to interact with D14 by pull-down assay. Interaction enhanced by GR24 in a dose-dependent manner.

mechanism: D14 recruits SCF-D3 E3 ligase to ubiquitinate D53 for 26S proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: D53 protein levels decreased rapidly upon GR24 treatment in wild-type seedlings. Mutations at Ser-His-Asp catalytic triad could attenuate the SL-induced degradation of D53.

mechanism: D3 is the F-box subunit of SCF complex that ubiquitinates D53; required for proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: SLs induce D53 degradation by the proteasome and abrogate its activity in promoting axillary bud outgrowth, in a D14- and D3-dependent manner.

mechanism: D53 binds IPA1 via direct protein-protein interaction and represses IPA1 transcriptional activation activity
evidence_sentence: D53 physically interacts with IPA1 in vivo and in vitro and suppresses the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1.

mechanism: D53 physically interacts with MOC1 to repress tiller bud outgrowth
evidence_sentence: D53 interacts with MOC1 to repress MOC1 activity in tiller regulation.

mechanism: D53 represses OsCKX9 expression; SL-induced D53 degradation derepresses OsCKX9
evidence_sentence: D53 represses OsCKX9; knockdown of D53 increases OsCKX9 expression.

mechanism: IPA1 directly binds GTAC motif in OsTB1 promoter via SBP domain and activates transcription
evidence_sentence: IPA1 could directly bind to the promoter of rice TEOSINTE BRANCHED1 via GTAC motif binding to suppress rice tillering.

mechanism: IPA1 directly binds GTAC element in D53 promoter and activates D53 transcription (negative feedback loop with D53 protein repressing IPA1)
evidence_sentence: IPA1 directly binds D53 promoter GTAC element and activates D53 transcription, forming negative feedback loop.

mechanism: IPA1 binds GTAC core motifs in OsDEP1 promoter, activating panicle morphology gene
evidence_sentence: IPA1 can directly bind to the promoter of DEP1 at different sites with GTAC core motifs.

mechanism: IPA1 directly binds OsLOG promoter; OsLOG is cytokinin activating enzyme
evidence_sentence: EMSA assays confirmed that IPA1 could directly target the promoter or first intron of LOG.

mechanism: IPA1 directly binds OsPIN1b promoter to activate auxin efflux carrier expression
evidence_sentence: EMSA confirmed IPA1 could directly target PIN1b promoter.

mechanism: IPA1 activates OsHK4 via TGGGCC motif binding with TCP cofactors PCF1/PCF2, completing positive feedback loop
evidence_sentence: IPA1 upregulates its expression through interactions with 2 TCP transcription factors; binds TGGGCC motifs in OsHK4 promoter via PCF1/PCF2 heterotrimers.

mechanism: OsmiR156 cleaves IPA1/OsSPL14 mRNA via miR156 recognition site in 3' region
evidence_sentence: One nucleotide substitution in the OsmiR156 recognition site perturbs OsmiR156-directed mRNA degradation; accumulation of OsSPL14 leads to ideal plant architecture.

mechanism: miR529a cleaves OsSPL14/IPA1 mRNA at the same site as miR156
evidence_sentence: MiR529a regulates panicle architecture and grain size through altering the expression of OsSPL2, OsSPL7, OsSPL14, OsSPL16, OsSPL17 and OsSPL18.

mechanism: IPI1 is a RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase that ubiquitinates IPA1; K48-linked chains in panicle drive degradation, K63 chains in shoot apex are non-proteolytic
evidence_sentence: IPI1 could promote targeting of the IPA1 complex with different polyubiquitin chains, and the diverse fate of IPA1 proteins could in turn regulate downstream genes; IPI1 promotes degradation of IPA1 in panicles.

mechanism: OsSHI1 physically interacts with IPA1 and reduces IPA1 DNA binding affinity, inhibiting transcriptional activation activity
evidence_sentence: OsSHI1 repressed the transcriptional activation activity of IPA1 via physical interaction.

mechanism: D27 is iron-containing beta-carotene isomerase, first step of SL biosynthesis converting all-trans to 9-cis-beta-carotene
evidence_sentence: 2'-epi-5-deoxystrigol was undetectable in d27 vs. wild-type root exudates.

mechanism: D10 encodes CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8 (CCD8) cleaving 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal to carlactone
evidence_sentence: D10 encodes a CAROTENOID CLEAVAGE DIOXYGENASE 8 (CCD8) involved in strigolactone biosynthesis.

mechanism: D17/HTD1 encodes CCD7 catalyzing cleavage of 9-cis-beta-carotene to 9-cis-beta-apo-10'-carotenal
evidence_sentence: D17/HIGH-TILLERING DWARF1 (HTD1) acts as enzyme in SL biosynthesis; rice D17 encodes CCD7.

mechanism: Rice MAX1 homologs (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100) convert carlactone to 4-deoxyorobanchol
evidence_sentence: Rice MAX1 (Os900) and other homologs convert carlactone to 4-deoxyorobanchol (4DO).

mechanism: Auxin signaling via AXR1-TIR1 pathway induces D10 expression; positive feedback from apical auxin to SL biosynthesis
evidence_sentence: Exogenous auxin up-regulates D10 in shoots.

mechanism: Auxin induces D17/HTD1 CCD7 expression (parallel to D10 regulation)
evidence_sentence: Auxin induces expression of D17 SL biosynthesis gene.

mechanism: OsTB1/FC1 is a TCP family transcription factor expressed in axillary buds that inhibits bud outgrowth; key branching integrator downstream of SL
evidence_sentence: OsTB1 functions as a negative regulator for lateral branching in rice.

mechanism: OsTB1 physically interacts with OsMADS57 and relieves OsMADS57-mediated repression of D14, activating D14 expression in buds
evidence_sentence: Interaction of OsMADS57 with OsTB1 reduced OsMADS57 inhibition of D14 transcription.

mechanism: OsMADS57 binds CArG motif in D14 promoter and directly suppresses D14 transcription
evidence_sentence: OsMADS57 bound to the CArG motif in the promoter and directly suppressed D14 expression.

mechanism: miR444a cleaves OsMADS57 mRNA
evidence_sentence: OsMADS57 gene is targeted and regulated negatively by miR444a.

mechanism: MOC1 is a GRAS-family transcription factor essential for axillary meristem initiation and bud outgrowth
evidence_sentence: MOC1 plays an essential role in the initiation of axillary bud formation; loss-of-function mutant moc1 exhibits a monoculm phenotype without tillers.

mechanism: MOC1 positively regulates OSH1 expression in developing axillary meristem
evidence_sentence: TE-OE lines had reduced OSH1 expression (inverse relationship via MOC1).

mechanism: TAD1 is Cdh1-type APC/C co-activator; recruits MOC1 to APC/C E3 ligase for cell-cycle-dependent ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: TAD1 interacts directly with MOC1; recruits MOC1 to APC/C and degrades MOC1 in cell-cycle-dependent manner; MOC1 accumulated to a higher level in tad1.

mechanism: TE is CDC20-type APC/C co-activator; mediates ubiquitin-26S proteasome degradation of MOC1
evidence_sentence: TE mediates the degradation of MOC1 by ubiquitin-26S proteasome pathway; te mutant drastically increased tiller number.

mechanism: SLR1 DELLA protein physically binds MOC1 and protects it from APC/C-mediated degradation
evidence_sentence: MOC1 is protected from degradation by binding to the DELLA protein SLR1; cell-free protein degradation assay showed degradation of MBP-MOC1 was inhibited by His-Trx-SLR1.

mechanism: GA binds GID1 receptor, which promotes ubiquitin-mediated degradation of DELLA SLR1 via SCF-GID2 F-box
evidence_sentence: GAs trigger the degradation of SLR1, leading to stem elongation.

mechanism: GA-GID1 complex targets NGR5 (AP2-TF) for proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: GA receptor GID1 targets NGR5 for proteolysis by the 26S proteasome.

mechanism: SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 binding, preventing NGR5 ubiquitination and degradation
evidence_sentence: SLR1 competes with NGR5 for GID1 and prevents NGR5 from GA-mediated proteolysis.

mechanism: NGR5 recruits PRC2 to OsTB1 promoter depositing H3K27me3 repressive mark
evidence_sentence: NGR5 directly interacts with PRC2 to form a repressive complex at the shoot branching inhibitory genes including OsTB1 in a nitrogen-dependent way.

mechanism: NGR5-PRC2 complex represses D14 via H3K27me3
evidence_sentence: NGR5 recruits PRC2 to branching inhibitory genes such as D14 for H3K27me3 repression.

mechanism: Nitrogen supply (nitrate sensing via NRT1.1B) promotes NGR5 accumulation and PRC2 recruitment
evidence_sentence: Nitrogen supply promotes tillering via NGR5 by chromatin modulation at branching-inhibitor genes.

mechanism: NRT1.1B is a nitrate transporter/transceptor; natural variation (indica vs japonica) affects N uptake and NGR5 activity
evidence_sentence: NRT1.1B is a nitrate transporter and sensor; indica allele shows enhanced nitrogen uptake and tiller response.

mechanism: OsTCP19 TCP TF directly binds DLT promoter and represses its transcription; inhibits N-responsive tillering
evidence_sentence: OsTCP19 directly represses the expression of DWARF AND LOW TILLERING (DLT); N-induced LBD37/LBD39 directly inhibit OsTCP19.

mechanism: DLT is GRAS-family BR signaling TF promoting tiller outgrowth
evidence_sentence: DLT (DWARF AND LOW TILLERING) plays positive roles in BR signaling and tiller outgrowth.

mechanism: N-induced OsLBD37 represses OsTCP19 transcription, relieving DLT repression and enabling N-induced tillering
evidence_sentence: Nitrogen-induced LATERAL ORGAN BOUNDARIES DOMAIN proteins OsLBD37 and OsLBD39 can directly repress OsTCP19.

mechanism: High nitrogen induces OsLBD37 transcription
evidence_sentence: Nitrogen induces OsLBD37/39 which then repress OsTCP19.

mechanism: OsCKX2/Gn1a irreversibly degrades cytokinin (N6-isopentenyladenine, trans-zeatin)
evidence_sentence: Gn1a encodes cytokinin oxidase/dehydrogenase (OsCKX2); reduced expression causes cytokinin accumulation in inflorescence meristems.

mechanism: OsCKX4 degrades cytokinin; represses tiller outgrowth
evidence_sentence: Multiple cytokinin oxidase genes including OsCKX4 are reported to repress tiller outgrowth through CK degradation.

mechanism: OsCKX9 degrades cytokinin; nuclear/cytosolic dual-localized enzyme
evidence_sentence: OsCKX9 functions as a cytosolic and nuclear dual-localized CK catabolic enzyme.

mechanism: OsCKX11 degrades cytokinin; coordinates source-sink relationship
evidence_sentence: OsCKX11 degrades cytokinin; knockout increases tiller number and alters source-sink relationship.

mechanism: Cytokinins are transported acropetally to axillary buds and promote bud outgrowth directly and via derepression of MOC1 target axillary meristem activity
evidence_sentence: Cytokinins are transported acropetally in xylem, and enter axillary buds and promote outgrowth.

mechanism: BAP cytokinin suppresses OsTB1/FC1 expression in a dose-dependent manner; independent of SL pathway
evidence_sentence: BAP treatment clearly suppressed FC1 expression in a concentration-dependent manner; negative control of FC1 expression by cytokinin is independent of the SL pathway.

mechanism: OsIPT genes (especially OsIPT4, OsIPT7) catalyze rate-limiting step of cytokinin biosynthesis (adenylate isopentenyltransferase)
evidence_sentence: Of eight IPTs implicated in cytokinin biosynthesis, OsIPT4 and OsIPT7 are major contributors in roots and shoots.

mechanism: Apical auxin signaling represses IPT genes in nodes, reducing cytokinin biosynthesis; apical dominance mechanism
evidence_sentence: Auxin down-regulates the expression of pea IPT family genes through AXR-TIR-dependent auxin signaling pathway.

mechanism: OsLOG converts inactive cytokinin nucleotides to active free-base form
evidence_sentence: OsLOG converts cytokinin nucleotides to active free bases; multiple OsLOG genes expressed with tissue specificity.

mechanism: CK binds OsHK4 histidine kinase CHASE domain activating phosphorelay
evidence_sentence: Cytokinin binds OsHK4 histidine kinase receptor activating downstream phosphorelay; D86-Q87 deletion abolishes receptor dimerization and CK binding.

mechanism: Activated OsHK4 phosphorylates OsAHP1/2 histidine phosphotransfer proteins
evidence_sentence: OsAHP1/2 are downstream phosphotransfer proteins in rice CK signaling connecting OsHKs to response regulators.

mechanism: OsAHP phosphorelay activates type-B OsRR21 response regulator for target gene transcription
evidence_sentence: The response of OsRR21 phosphorylation to CK was diminished in the pal1 mutant; OsAHP mediates this relay.

mechanism: Type-B OsRR21 directly binds CK response elements in IPA1 promoter activating transcription
evidence_sentence: OsRR21 directly activates IPA1 by binding to 10 CK response elements in IPA1 promoter (Y1H, ChIP-qPCR validated).

mechanism: SL induces OsCKX9 via D53 degradation and derepression; primary SL-responsive gene
evidence_sentence: SL can induce the expression of OsCKX9 to down-regulate CK content; GR24 treatment activates OsCKX9.

mechanism: OsPIN1a auxin efflux transporter maintains apical dominance by polar auxin transport; knockout increases tiller number
evidence_sentence: Knockdown of OsPIN1/1b decrease the number of crown root and increase tiller number.

mechanism: OsPIN1b auxin efflux transporter, knockout shows increased tillers
evidence_sentence: Knockout mutants of OsPIN1b increase tiller numbers.

mechanism: OsPIN2 OE increases tiller number; enhances shoot-to-root auxin transport
evidence_sentence: Overexpression of OsPIN2 leads to increased tiller numbers, angle and shorter plant.

mechanism: OsPIN9 ammonium-induced auxin efflux transporter promotes tiller bud outgrowth
evidence_sentence: Overexpression of OsPIN9 result in increased tillering; knockout result in decreased tillering.

mechanism: Ammonium specifically induces OsPIN9 expression (not nitrate)
evidence_sentence: OsPIN9 expression is induced by ammonium, a major source of nitrogen.

mechanism: Apical auxin signaling activates OsTB1/FC1 expression in axillary buds (conserved with Arabidopsis BRC1)
evidence_sentence: Auxin-induced apical dominance requires the activity of BRC1; same mechanism in rice via OsTB1/FC1.

mechanism: OsTIR1 is auxin receptor F-box, binds IAA and promotes Aux/IAA degradation; not a transcriptional target but catalytic activation
evidence_sentence: OsTIR1/OsAFB family members are auxin receptors; exhibit partial redundancy in regulating yield, tillering, plant height.

mechanism: OsTIR1-auxin complex ubiquitinates OsIAA repressors for proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: OsIAA proteins are degraded upon auxin perception via TIR1/AFB complex.

mechanism: OsIAA repressors bind OsARF transcription factors inhibiting auxin-responsive gene expression; auxin signaling destabilizes IAAs to derepress ARFs
evidence_sentence: OsIAA12 directly interacts with OsARF17 to regulate leaf inclination.

mechanism: OsCCA1 circadian MYB-like TF binds CBS element in OsTB1 promoter
evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS elements in OsTB1 promoter activating expression.

mechanism: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D14 promoter
evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D14 promoter; ChIP-qPCR confirmed.

mechanism: OsCCA1 binds CBS in D10 promoter
evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS in D10 promoter.

mechanism: OsCCA1 binds CBS/EE elements in IPA1 promoter
evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS/EE elements in IPA1 promoter.

mechanism: OsPRR1 pseudo-response regulator represses OsCCA1 in reciprocal feedback loop
evidence_sentence: OsPRR1 forms reciprocal feedback loop with OsCCA1 (negative repression).

mechanism: OsCCA1 binds CBS in OsPRR1 promoter and represses expression (part of evening loop)
evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 directly binds CBS on OsPRR1 promoter (negative regulation in reciprocal feedback).

mechanism: Sucrose signaling stabilizes D53 protein (antagonizes SL-induced degradation)
evidence_sentence: Sucrose promotes D53 accumulation and tillering in rice; antagonizes SL-mediated D53 degradation.

mechanism: Sugars are primary signals for bud outgrowth; sugar status antagonizes SL inhibition
evidence_sentence: Sugars are key regulators of axillary bud elongation; sucrose restricts growth of axillary buds when depleted.

mechanism: Photosynthetic sugars downregulate OsCCA1 in roots and buds (tissue-dependent)
evidence_sentence: Photosynthetic sugars negatively regulate OsCCA1 expression in roots/buds.

mechanism: MOC2 encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase1 for sucrose biosynthesis pathway
evidence_sentence: MOC2 encodes a fructose-1,6-bisphosphatase1 responsible for the sucrose biosynthesis pathway.

mechanism: LAX1 bHLH TF required for axillary meristem initiation; non-cell-autonomous movement essential
evidence_sentence: LAX1 is required for AM initiation throughout plant's lifetime; lax1 mutants fail to progress into AM formation.

mechanism: LAX2 nuclear protein physically interacts with LAX1 to promote axillary meristem
evidence_sentence: LAX2 nuclear cofactor acts together with LAX1 to maintain axillary meristems; lax1 lax2 double mutant completely lacks tillers.

mechanism: LAX1 and LAX2 physically interact; cooperative nuclear function
evidence_sentence: Yeast two-hybrid and in vitro binding assays demonstrated that LAX1 and LAX2 physically interact.

mechanism: TAB1 (OsWUS) is rice ortholog of WUSCHEL; required for axillary meristem initiation via premeristem stem cell maintenance
evidence_sentence: tab1-1 produces no tillers even in 2-month-old shoots; TAB1 required to initiate axillary meristem development.

mechanism: TAB1 required for proper OSH1 expression in premeristem zone
evidence_sentence: TAB1 is required for the proper expression of OSH1 in premeristem zone.

mechanism: FON2 CLV3-like peptide restricts TAB1 expression domain via CLAVATA-like receptor signaling
evidence_sentence: FON2 restricts the stem cell population by moderately repressing TAB1 expression.

mechanism: MOC3 (TAB1/OsWUS) physically interacts with MOC1 to activate FON1
evidence_sentence: Physical interaction between MOC1 and MOC3 can up-regulate the expression of FON1.

mechanism: FON1 (CLAVATA1 ortholog) specifically affects tiller bud elongation; fon1 forms buds but fails to elongate
evidence_sentence: fon1 mutant forms them normally but fails to elongate tiller buds.

mechanism: OsBZR1 recruits D53 to OsTB1 promoter repressing transcription; D53-BZR1 complex under BR signaling
evidence_sentence: OsBZR1 recruits D53 to repress the expression of OsTB1 forming D53-BZR1 complex.

mechanism: BR stabilizes OsBZR1 via inhibition of OsBIN2 kinase (dephosphorylated BZR1)
evidence_sentence: BR is known to stabilize the OsBZR1 protein.

mechanism: SL treatment triggers OsBZR1 degradation
evidence_sentence: SL treatment triggers the degradation of OsBZR1.

mechanism: OsTB2 japonica allele reduces D14 expression via interaction with OsTB1 interfering with positive D14 regulation
evidence_sentence: japonica OsTB2-3bp+ allele reduces the expression of D14 via interaction with OsTB1.

mechanism: OsTB2 physically binds OsTB1 and antagonizes its function
evidence_sentence: OsTB2 protein directly interacts with OsTB1 and interferes with positive regulatory effect of OsTB1 on D14.

mechanism: OsTB1/FC1 directly binds GT1 (GRASSY TILLER1) promoter
evidence_sentence: Upon up-regulation of OsTB1/FC1, its protein enhances expression of GRASSY TILLER1 (GT1) by directly binding.

mechanism: GT1 regulates NCED1 (ABA biosynthesis) to induce bud dormancy
evidence_sentence: GT1 is likely to regulate genes such as OsNCED1 that encode abscisic acid biosynthesis enzymes.

mechanism: NCED1 (9-cis-epoxycarotenoid dioxygenase) is rate-limiting ABA biosynthesis enzyme
evidence_sentence: NCED1 encodes abscisic acid biosynthesis enzymes.

mechanism: ABA promotes bud dormancy
evidence_sentence: ABA involved in axillary bud dormancy and suppression of outgrowth.

mechanism: DST zinc finger protein binds DBS motif in OsCKX2 promoter and activates transcription; reg1 allele loses activation but retains binding
evidence_sentence: ChIP assay confirms DST binds DBS motif in OsCKX2 promoter; DST-REG1 increases luciferase activity driven by OsCKX2 promoter.

mechanism: OsNLP4 RWP-RK domain binds NRE motif in OsNiR promoter; nitrate signaling
evidence_sentence: OsNLP4 RWP-RK domain binds NRE motif in OsNiR promoter enhancing transcription.

mechanism: Nitrate triggers nuclear retention of NLP4 for transcription activation
evidence_sentence: Nitrate triggers nuclear retention of NLP4 and transcription activation.

mechanism: OsNiR reduces nitrite to ammonia, enabling N assimilation that supports tiller growth
evidence_sentence: OsNiR converts nitrite to ammonia; enhanced nitrogen assimilation supports tiller growth.

mechanism: P-sufficient conditions repress SL biosynthesis genes D27, D10, D17; P-deficiency elevates them
evidence_sentence: Under P-deficient conditions, expression levels of SL biosynthesis genes were elevated; P-deficiency elevates SL content.

mechanism: P deficiency elevates D10/CCD8 expression
evidence_sentence: P deficiency elevates D10 expression driving SL biosynthesis.

mechanism: P deficiency elevates D17 expression
evidence_sentence: P deficiency elevates D17 expression.

mechanism: Strigolactone inhibits axillary bud outgrowth via the D14-D53-IPA1-OsTB1 cascade and via OsCKX9-mediated CK degradation; encoded here as direct summary edge (the detailed cascade is also represented)
evidence_sentence: SLs move to axillary buds and inhibit their outgrowth.

mechanism: IPA1 accumulation (ipa1-1D) reduces tiller number (via OsTB1 activation); summary edge — the detailed IPA1→OsTB1→Tiller cascade is also represented
evidence_sentence: Accumulation of OsSPL14 leads to the formation of ideal plant architecture with decreased tiller number and increased plant height and panicle branches.

mechanism: NPF7.7 nitrate/ammonium transporter promotes effective tillers
evidence_sentence: NPF7.7 can increase the inflow of both NO3- and NH4+, thereby promoting the number of effective tillers.

mechanism: OsAAP5 amino acid permease affects cytokinin biosynthesis precursors
evidence_sentence: OsAAP5 can affect rice tiller number and yield by regulating cytokinin biosynthesis.

mechanism: GS1;2 (glutamine synthetase) promotes CK production via primary N assimilation
evidence_sentence: GS1;2 functions in primary assimilation of NH4+ and promotes tiller bud growth by CK production.

mechanism: SLR1 increases tiller number via MOC1 protein stabilization and NGR5 stabilization
evidence_sentence: SLR1-OE: tiller number was increased; SLR1 protects MOC1 from degradation.

mechanism: GA inhibits tillering via SLR1 and NGR5 degradation; summary edge
evidence_sentence: GA-deficient and GA-signaling mutants produce more tillers than wild type; sd1 allele increases tillers.

mechanism: OsARF12 activates OsCDKF;2 for cell cycle progression
evidence_sentence: OsARF12 targets OsCDKF;2; redundantly controls tiller angle with OsARF17/25.

mechanism: SL promotes depletion of PIN1 from plasma membrane via endocytosis, reducing auxin transport
evidence_sentence: OsPIN1/1b responds to nitric oxide and strigolactone in rice; SL-mediated PIN1 depletion reported in dicots.

mechanism: Decapitation removes apical auxin source, releasing axillary buds from apical dominance
evidence_sentence: Decapitation removes apical auxin source; rapid sugar flux precedes auxin depletion at buds.

mechanism: Apical auxin maintains bud dormancy via cytokinin suppression, SL biosynthesis activation, and BRC1/OsTB1 activation; summary edge
evidence_sentence: Auxins inhibit axillary bud outgrowth and do not migrate to axillary buds.

mechanism: miR444a OE reduces tillers via OsMADS57 mRNA cleavage (summary edge)
evidence_sentence: OsMIR444a-overexpressing lines: suppressed OsMADS57 expression, fewer tillers.

mechanism: SL signaling indirectly activates OsTB1 via D53 degradation derepressing IPA1; OsTB1 expression increases under SL
evidence_sentence: FC1 expression was not significantly affected by GR24 treatment directly — SL acts post-transcriptionally on FC1 function; indirect activation through IPA1 cascade.

mechanism: High N represses OsTB1 via NGR5-PRC2 H3K27me3 deposition
evidence_sentence: Nitrogen fertilizer causes the downregulation of OsTB1 to promote tiller formation.

mechanism: High N represses OsTCP19 via LBD37/39 induction
evidence_sentence: OsTCP19 expression is induced at low nitrogen levels but is repressed at higher concentrations.

mechanism: N sufficiency represses SL biosynthesis genes D10, D17, D27
evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D10, D17, D27; high N represses them.

mechanism: N sufficiency represses D17/HTD1 SL biosynthesis
evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D17; high N represses.

mechanism: N sufficiency represses D27 SL biosynthesis
evidence_sentence: N-deficient conditions upregulate D27; high N represses.

mechanism: Light signal entrains OsCCA1 expression (canonical circadian clock input)
evidence_sentence: OsCCA1 shows morning-peak circadian expression entrained by light, and integrates sugar signaling in tiller regulation.

mechanism: Photoperiod regulates OsPRR1 evening expression
evidence_sentence: OsPRR1 evening-peak expression is photoperiod-dependent in rice clock.

mechanism: OsTAR2 is TAA/TAR aminotransferase; first step of IAA biosynthesis from tryptophan to IPA
evidence_sentence: OsTAR2 catalyzes the first step of tryptophan-dependent IAA biosynthesis in rice.

mechanism: OsYUCCA enzymes catalyze rate-limiting step of IAA biosynthesis (IPA to IAA)
evidence_sentence: OsYUCCAs are rate-limiting enzymes in auxin synthesis; multiple members contribute to tiller angle and architecture.

mechanism: Physical interaction between OsTB1 and OsMADS57 relieves OsMADS57 repression on D14
evidence_sentence: Interaction of OsMADS57 with OsTB1 reduced OsMADS57 inhibition of D14 transcription.

mechanism: MOC1 positively affects axillary meristem gene network including LAX1
evidence_sentence: MOC1 works together with LAX1 in axillary meristem formation; lax1 moc1 double mutant shows enhanced AM suppression.

mechanism: SLs exuded from roots induce hyphal branching of arbuscular mycorrhizal fungi
evidence_sentence: SLs exudated from roots induce hyphal branching of AM fungi.

mechanism: OsPRR1-OE reduces tillers (summary of clock effect via OsCCA1 repression)
evidence_sentence: OsPRR1-OE: reduced tiller numbers.

mechanism: OsCCA1 represses tillering via OsTB1/D14/IPA1 activation
evidence_sentence: oscca1 mutant shows more tillers and delayed flowering; OsCCA1-OE fewer tillers.

mechanism: TAD1 inhibits tillering via MOC1 degradation (summary)
evidence_sentence: tad1 mutant showed increased tiller number; TAD1-OX reduced tiller number.

mechanism: TE inhibits tillering via MOC1 degradation (summary)
evidence_sentence: te mutant displays drastically increased tiller number.

mechanism: D53 accumulation promotes tillering (via IPA1 repression and SL signaling block); summary
evidence_sentence: d53 mutant shows ~3x wild-type tiller number due to D53 accumulation.

mechanism: OsCKX2 depletes CK reducing tillering (summary)
evidence_sentence: Reduced OsCKX2 expression causes cytokinin accumulation and increased tillers; Gn1a reduced-function allele is high-tillering.

mechanism: OsLAX1 is a synonym for LAX1 (LAX PANICLE1) in rice
evidence_sentence: LAX1 (also called OsLAX1 or LAX PANICLE1) regulates axillary meristem formation.

mechanism: Sucrose synthesis generates trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P) signaling molecule
evidence_sentence: Mounting evidence indicates that sucrose effects on branching are mediated by trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P).

mechanism: T6P signaling represses BRC1/OsTB1 expression
evidence_sentence: Sucrose/T6P signaling partially represses PsBRC1 (OsTB1 ortholog) expression in peripheral buds.

mechanism: NRT1.1B-dependent phosphorylation promotes NGR5 stability (nitrate-sensing-to-chromatin)
evidence_sentence: NRT1.1B natural variation in indica enhances N uptake signaling that converges on NGR5 activity.

mechanism: GID1 is GA receptor; GA-GID1 complex targets SLR1 for SCF-GID2-mediated proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: GID1 is a soluble GA receptor; GA-GID1 complex promotes SLR1 interaction with F-box SCF-GID2 for degradation.

mechanism: GA-GID1 complex directly targets NGR5 for proteasomal degradation
evidence_sentence: GA receptor GID1 targets NGR5 for proteolysis by the 26S proteasome.

mechanism: OsGA20ox2 (SD1) catalyzes multiple oxidation steps in GA biosynthesis
evidence_sentence: sd1 allele is a null mutation in GA20ox2; sd1 plants have reduced GA levels.

mechanism: CK signaling induces type-A OsRRs as primary response genes (negative feedback on CK signaling)
evidence_sentence: Most type-A OsRR genes rapidly upregulated by cytokinin (peak 30-60 min).

mechanism: Type-A OsRRs provide negative feedback on CK signaling
evidence_sentence: Type-A OsRRs act as negative regulators of CK signaling (classical feedback).

mechanism: D53 contains EAR motifs and recruits TOPLESS/TPR co-repressors for transcriptional silencing
evidence_sentence: D53 contains three putative EAR motifs and interacts with TOPLESS-RELATED PROTEINS (TPR1, TPR2, TPR3).

mechanism: TPL co-repressor recruited by D53 represses IPA1-bound target genes
evidence_sentence: D53-TPR interaction likely represses IPA1 transcriptional activation by delivering TPR co-repressors to IPA1-bound loci.

mechanism: BR signaling activates DLT expression/function in tiller promotion
evidence_sentence: DLT plays positive roles in brassinosteroid signaling in rice; BR pathway activates DLT.

mechanism: SL-bound D14 directly binds D3 F-box protein to form D14-D3 complex required for D53 recruitment and ubiquitination; D3 C-terminal alpha-helix switches to engaged form upon binding
evidence_sentence: GR24 induces a direct interaction between D14 and D3 measured by AlphaScreen assay; D14 surface residues required for D14-D3 interaction are clustered at the open lid domain surrounding the entrance of the ligand binding pocket.

mechanism: NGR5 binds IPA1/OsSPL14 promoter and recruits PRC2 complex to deposit H3K27me3 mark; ChIP-qPCR direct binding confirmed; nitrogen dose-dependent
evidence_sentence: ChIP-qPCR analysis showed that NGR5 can directly bind to the promoter region of D14 and SPL14, and the binding strength increased with increasing nitrogen concentration.

mechanism: Photosynthesis-derived sucrose activates NGR5 expression at the shoot base, providing carbon-nitrogen integration for tiller bud elongation
evidence_sentence: The expression of nitrogen-mediated tiller growth response 5 (NGR5) was remarkably decreased in shoot base of dtn1-2, which can be activated by sucrose treatment.

mechanism: DTN1 (Decreased Tiller Number 1) encodes fructose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase and is required for photosynthesis and sucrose accumulation in shoot base / axillary buds
evidence_sentence: Deficiency of DTN1 results in reduced photosynthetic rate and decreased contents of sucrose and other sugars in both leaves and axillary buds.

mechanism: OsVIL2 chromatin-interacting factor binds OsTB1 promoter and represses OsTB1 expression via chromatin modification, releasing axillary bud outgrowth
evidence_sentence: Expression of OsTB1 was increased in osvil2 mutants. ChIP assays showed that OsVIL2 binds to the promoter region of OsTB1 in wild-type rice; tiller number of double mutant osvil2 ostb1 was similar to that of ostb1 (osvil2 epistatic to ostb1).

mechanism: OsGATA8 transcription factor represses OsTCP19 (negative regulator of tillering), promoting productive tiller formation under N-replete conditions
evidence_sentence: OsGATA8 promotes tiller formation by repressing the transcription of OsTCP19, a negative modulator of tillering.

mechanism: OsGATA8 directly represses OsAMT3.2 ammonium transporter; the OsGATA8-H haplotype balances N uptake with tiller productivity
evidence_sentence: OsGATA8 negatively regulates nitrogen uptake by repressing transcription of the ammonium transporter gene OsAMT3.2.

mechanism: OsGATA8-H haplotype increases productive tiller proportion by simultaneously gating N uptake (via OsAMT3.2 repression) and de-repressing tiller via OsTCP19 repression
evidence_sentence: The OsGATA8-H haplotype is identified as a high-NUE haplotype with enhanced nitrogen uptake and a higher proportion of productive tillers.

mechanism: Os1900 (one of five rice MAX1-like paralogs) catalyzes carlactone-to-carlactonoic acid conversion together with Os5100; Os1900 is the N-fertilization-responsive paralog
evidence_sentence: Os1900 together with another MAX1-like gene, Os5100, play a critical role in controlling the conversion of carlactone into carlactonoic acid during strigolactone biosynthesis and tillering in rice.

mechanism: Fertilization (N supply) transcriptionally upregulates Os1900 promoter; promoter mutations that disrupt N-induction increase tiller numbers under low fertilizer
evidence_sentence: Fertilization controls tiller number in rice through transcriptional regulation of Os1900, and a few promoter mutations alone can increase tiller numbers and grain yields even under minor-fertilizer conditions.

mechanism: MOC1 physically interacts with MOC3 (OsWUS/TAB1) and acts as a transcriptional co-activator; MOC1 cannot bind FON1 promoter alone but cooperates with MOC3 binding
evidence_sentence: MOC3 is able to directly bind the promoter of FON1 and subsequently activate FON1 expression, while MOC1 functions as a co-activator of MOC3 but could not directly bind the FON1 promoter.

mechanism: MOC3 (TAB1/OsWUS) is required for axillary meristem initiation in cooperation with MOC1; loss of MOC3 prevents tiller formation (summary edge)
evidence_sentence: MOC1 and MOC3 cooperatively promote tiller bud outgrowth by activating FON1 expression in rice.

mechanism: T20 encodes ζ-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO) acting upstream of D27 in the carotenoid pathway; required for SL precursor supply
evidence_sentence: The T20 gene encodes a chloroplast ζ-carotene isomerase (Z-ISO), which is involved in the biosynthesis of carotenoids and their metabolites, strigolactone (SL) and abscisic acid (ABA).

mechanism: T20/Z-ISO supplies carotenoid precursors for ABA biosynthesis (parallel to SL biosynthesis arm)
evidence_sentence: T20 encodes Z-ISO involved in the biosynthesis of carotenoids and their metabolites, strigolactone (SL) and abscisic acid (ABA).

mechanism: rac-GR24 (synthetic SL) induces OsHOX12 expression in shoot base; OsHOX12 then transactivates OsNCED1 to drive ABA biosynthesis
evidence_sentence: rac-GR24 stimulated expression of OsNCED1 through induction of OsHOX12, promoting ABA biosynthesis in shoot base.

mechanism: OsHOX12 homeodomain TF directly induces OsNCED1 expression to promote ABA biosynthesis (SL → OsHOX12 → NCED1 → ABA)
evidence_sentence: rac-GR24 stimulated expression of OsNCED1 through induction of OsHOX12, promoting ABA biosynthesis in shoot base.

mechanism: ABA represses SL biosynthesis genes (D10, D17, D27); ABA biosynthetic mutants show elevated SL biosynthesis (negative feedback from ABA to SL)
evidence_sentence: ABA treatment significantly repressed SL biosynthesis and ABA biosynthetic mutants displayed elevated SL biosynthesis.

mechanism: OsHK3/OsHK5 are paralogous CK histidine kinase receptors with partial redundancy with OsHK4; phosphorylate OsAHP1/2 in cytokinin signalling
evidence_sentence: Similar to its homologs OHK3/OsHK5 and OHK5/OsHK6, knockout of OHK4 resulted in shorter panicles with reduced numbers of branches and grains due to the reduction in SAM (shoot apical meristem) and IM (inflorescence meristem) activity.

mechanism: Cytokinin binds OsHK3 CHASE domain (paralogous to OsHK4) activating histidine kinase phosphorelay
evidence_sentence: Rice has 5 histidine kinase (HK) genes (OsHK1-4, OsHKL1) that function as cytokinin-response histidine protein kinases; CK binds CHASE-domain receptors.

mechanism: OsDRM1 (Dormancy-Associated Gene 1) is a downstream marker of bud dormancy in rice; predominantly expressed in dormant axillary buds where OsTB1/FC1 is high
evidence_sentence: OsDRM1 is predominantly expressed in dormant axillary buds, while it is weakly expressed in growing buds, indicating that OsDRM1 gene can be used as a molecular marker for bud dormancy in rice.

mechanism: OsDRM1 OE delays axillary bud outgrowth by suppressing cell-cycle progression; acts as a growth repressor by modulating auxin response
evidence_sentence: Overexpression of OsDRM1 in transgenic plants delayed axillary bud outgrowth by suppressing cell division within the buds.

mechanism: Os1900 is one of the five rice OsMAX1 paralogs (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100); the OsMAX1 generic node aggregates this family
evidence_sentence: Rice has five MAX1-like genes (Os900, Os1400, Os1500, Os1900, Os5100) catalysing distinct steps in strigolactone biosynthesis.
